back_translation: At that time, some Pharisees and scribes who came from Jerusalem approached Jesus and asked Him,
"Why do Your disciples transgress the tradition of the ancestors? For they do not wash their hands ritually before eating food."
He answered them, "Then why do you transgress the commandment of God for the sake of your tradition?
For God said, 'Honor your father and mother,' and 'Whoever curses his father or mother must be put to death.'
But you say instead, 'Whoever says to his father or mother, "Whatever you might have benefited from me has already been offered to God,"'
'such a person need not honor his father [or mother] .' In this way, you have nullified the word of God for the sake of your tradition.
You hypocrites! Isaiah prophesied correctly about you, saying,
'These people honor Me with their lips, but their hearts are far from Me.
They worship Me in vain, teaching the commandments of men as doctrine.'"
Then He called the crowd to Him and said, "Listen and understand.
It is not what enters the mouth that defiles a person, but what comes out of the mouth — that is what defiles a person."
Then the disciples approached Him and said, "Do You know that the Pharisees, upon hearing that statement, felt offended?"
He answered, "Every plant that My Father in heaven has not planted will be uprooted.
Let them be. They are blind people leading the blind. If a blind person leads the blind, both will fall into a pit."
Peter said to Him, "Please explain this parable to us."
He said, "Are you also still lacking understanding?
Do you not yet understand that everything that enters the mouth goes into the stomach and then is expelled to the latrine?
But what comes out of the mouth comes from the heart, and those are what defile a person.
For out of the heart come evil thoughts, murders, adulteries, sexual immoralities, thefts, false testimonies, and slanders.
These are what defile a person. As for eating food without ritually washing the hands — that does not defile a person at all."
Then Jesus went out from that place to the regions of Tyre and Sidon.
Behold, a Canaanite woman came out from that region and cried out loudly, "Have mercy on me, O Lord, Son of David. My daughter is possessed by a demon and suffering greatly."
But He did not answer her a single word. His disciples came and said to Him, "Please send her away, for she keeps crying out after us incessantly."
He answered, "I was not sent for anyone else except the lost sheep of the house of Israel."
But she came and prostrated herself before Him, saying, "O Lord, please help me."
He answered, "It is not fitting to take the children's food and throw it to the little dogs."
She said, "Indeed so, O Lord, but even the little dogs eat the crumbs that fall from their masters' table."
Then Jesus answered her, "O woman, your faith is great indeed. Let it be done for you as you wish." And in that very hour her daughter was restored to health.
Jesus departed from there, traveling along the shore of the Sea of Galilee. Then He went up onto a mountain and stayed there.
Large crowds came to Him, bringing with them the mute, the blind, the lame, the disabled, and many others, and they laid them down at His feet, and He healed them.
The crowd was amazed when they saw the mute speaking, the disabled made whole again, the lame walking, and the blind seeing. They glorified the God of Israel.

Jesus called His disciples to Him and said, "I have compassion on the crowd, for they have been with Me three days and have nothing to eat. I do not want to send them away while still hungry, lest they collapse from weakness along the way."
The disciples said to Him, "In such a desolate place, where can we find enough bread to feed such a large crowd?"
Jesus asked, "How many loaves do you have?" They answered, "Seven, and a few small fish."
Then He ordered the crowd to sit down on the ground.
He took the seven loaves and the fish, gave thanks, broke them, and gave them to the disciples, and the disciples distributed them to the crowd.
Everyone ate until they were satisfied, and the leftover broken pieces they gathered up, filling seven large baskets.
Those who ate were four thousand men, not counting women and children.
When He had sent the crowd away, He got into a boat and went to the region of Magadan.
